commands.review_summary:
This PR removes the bundled-origin filter from non-secret API key marker discovery so manifest-declared markers from installed/external plugins are recognized, updates the manifest docs, and adds a focused unit test.

PR surface: Source -2, Tests +25, Docs 0. Total +23 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main only reads manifest nonSecretAuthMarkers when plugin.origin is bundled, while installed/global manifest records are otherwise discoverable. I did not run a live installed-plugin repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review should verify the external plugin auth-marker contract and request real installed-plugin proof; there is no narrow ClawSweeper code repair from this review.

target.title: fix: honor plugin auth markers from manifests
target.body:
## Summary
- Carries plugin auth markers from external manifests into model auth marker handling.
- Updates manifest docs for the external auth marker behavior.
- Keeps the fork-main fix available while opening this clean upstream review branch.

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/model-auth-markers.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

## Fork Context
This is split from `LDMB123/openclaw` fork `main`, which keeps accepted fork fixes rebased on top of upstream `main`. This PR branch contains only the plugin auth marker fix for upstream review.
target.author: ldmb123
